forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Patchy fog and a slight chance of showers and thunderstorms between 9pm and 10pm, then patchy fog and a slight chance of showers and thunderstorms between 10pm and midnight, then patchy fog and showers and thunderstorms likely between midnight and 2am, then patchy fog and a chance of showers and thunderstorms between 2am and 5am, then patchy fog and a chance of showers and thunderstorms. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 52. East wind around 5 mph. Chance of precipitation is 60%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.

forecast.name: Thursday
forecast.detailedForecast: Patchy fog and a chance of showers and thunderstorms before 7am, then patchy fog and a slight chance of showers and thunderstorms. Partly sunny. High near 70, with temperatures falling to around 64 in the afternoon. South wind 3 to 9 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%.

weather_report:
As night settles over Boston, a delicate curtain of fog begins to weave through the streets, mingling with the remnants of the day. The air hangs heavy with the promise of showers and thunderstorms, casting an ethereal quality across the city. With temperatures dipping to around fifty-two, an easy-going east wind comes in at five miles per hour, wrapping the evening in a cool embrace. As the hours stretch into the night and into the early morning, there's a 60% chance of rainfall, hinting at the possibility of a gentle patter against windows and rooftops. If venturing out, consider layering up; perhaps a light jacket with a hood, to guard against both the chill and a potential sprinkle.

Tomorrow beckons with a sense of renewal. Early morning fog may linger, cloaking the landscape in mystery, yet as the sun strives to break through, temperatures rise to a comfortable seventy, transforming into a pleasant afternoon hovering around sixty-four. A slight breeze from the south invites leisurely walks in the park, perhaps meandering along the Charles River or enjoying the blossoming tulips in the local gardens. With a 30% chance of lingering showers, keeping an umbrella handy will be wise, though the likelihood of seeing the sun could sway the day toward delight. Choose a relaxed pair of shoes, and perhaps a light cardigan, to navigate both the dampness of this spring night and the promise of a surprisingly charming Thursday.
